script.dialogue: There is still so much left to be done…
Evening, Hubert. Keeping your usual hours, I see.
Ah, Gautier. Taking the late guard shift again?
Yeah. I’ve been finding it difficult to sleep recently anyway, so I may as well make use of the time.
If you’re struggling to remain well-rested, I’m sure we could have one of our medics fix you a sleeping draught. You’re of little use to us if you’re exhausted.
I’ll be fine! You worry far too much.
On the contrary, it is my job to ensure everyone in our army is able to contribute effectively.
Yes, I’m well aware—you’re only saying it every other sentence. Actually… Repeat it a few more times and my sleeping issues just might go away.
Very funny. I’d almost begun to miss your backtalk.
Heh. I’ve always believed you were far more fond of me than you cared to admit. I can have that effect on people.
Let’s not get ahead of ourselves. It is not fondness so much as it is fascination. Despite your reprehensible behavior, I cannot deny that you’ve made great efforts to curb your shortcomings.
And as grating as your personality is, the truth of the matter is that you made—and continue to make—great sacrifices by staying here.

If this is about me turning my back on the Kingdom again, that was hardly a sacrifice. If anything, the decision I made back then was self-serving and impulsive. But I can’t do anything to change it, so…
If it were such a simple matter, I suspect you would have deserted long ago. Something else made you stay.
And you want to know what it was, clearly. I guess I really do fascinate you.
The truth is that I’m still figuring it out myself. But... You said something to me a long time ago, about irredeemable nobles misusing their power.
I believe I also made it clear I did not consider you one of them.
Which almost makes me worse in a way, right? Having the ability to change for the better and choosing not to.

It got to a point where I was so certain that if I carried on as I was—graduated the Academy and went back home to do exactly what was expected of me, no matter how much I resented it—I would become just as irredeemable.
Frankly, there were no good options. Betraying my country isn’t what I’d call honorable.
Honor… Such a flawed concept. One’s loyalty should be earned, not assumed.
Is that how it is with you and Edelgard? It’s hard to tell, sometimes, with how obsessive you are.

She and I went through a similar dilemma as you, in a way. Realizing the rot at the center of our institutions, understanding that we could either follow that toxic path or cut our own. Obviously you know what we chose.
You started a war.
Like you said: there were no good options.
Ugh. That’s life sometimes, isn’t it? But we have to keep walking.
Precisely.
Thank you for sharing your thoughts with me, Gautier. They were very… Enlightening.
Sure, let’s call it that. If you’re ever bored again in the future, I’d be happy to keep you entertained.
That’s not—you know what, nevermind. There is only so much of your nonsense I can take in one sitting. Farewell.
Hubert leaves the scene.
My nonsense, he says. Has he ever looked in a mirror?
Sylvain and Hubert have reached Support Rank A+.
